description: Quick Export Link This dataset contains the active business licenses registered with the City of Little Rock. Anyone who is operating a business within the City of Little Rock is required to obtain a business license or privilege license. City of Little Rock Rental Housing Registration Program also requires a registered agent, which are also contained in this data set under real estate.

description: This dataset contains Police Calls for Service from December 2020 to Year to Date. The addresses have been generalized to block level and offset to protect the privacy of our residents. Incidents involving sensitive call types have had the addressing removed. Those include: Assist Medical, Child Abandonment, Child Abuse Report, Death, Harassing Communication, Police - Overdose, Police - Suicide, Rape, Sexual Abuse Report, Terroristic Threatening and Vacation Home Report. description: This dataset contains a list of the homes that the City has provided down payment assistance for purchase. The City of Little Rock has allocated funds to assist low and moderate income first time homebuyers to purchase owner occupancy residential properties within the city limits. Applicants must be pre-approved by a lender. The program funds are provided by HUD through the HOME Investment Partnership Program.

name: Information Technology Scheduled Maintenance with Outage
description: This dataset tracks Information Technology scheduled maintenance that requires some network systems to be taken off-line.

name: Food Desert Task Force Numbers

columns.name: Would you be willing to try an online food-purchasing program?
Which is more of an issue to grocery shopping for you and your family?
How do you rate the quality and value (cost) of the food available at the stores where you most frequently purchase your groceries?
Within the past 12 months, the food you bought just didn’t last and you didn’t have the money to get more?
How do you access the internet and/or email? (Please check all that apply):
If you do not shop for food online, why not? (Please check all that apply.)
Within the past 12 months, you worried that your food would run out before you got the money to buy more?
If yes, what concerns do you have about food being delivered to your home?
How many times in the past 12 months have you shopped for groceries ONLINE for pick-up or delivery?
How do you rate the variety of food available at the store where you most frequently purchase your groceries? (Note: an answer of “very good” means you can get all of the products you want.)
The distance I need to travel to get the food I need for myself/my family is challenging.
How often do you go shopping for the major foods for your household? (DO NOT include times when you buy only a few items)
When you go grocery shopping, how long does it take you, including traveling to the store, shopping, and returning?
How do you currently get to the grocery store? (Check all that apply)
Do you have concerns about food being delivered to your home?
In the past week, how much money have you spent on groceries for your household? (DO NOT count money spent on cigarettes or alcoholic beverages. PLEASE include purchases made with SNAP)

description: This dataset displays information from the Food Desert task force questionnaire.

name: Positive Prevention Programs
description: This Dataset contains the list, location and overall details about the Positive Prevention Programs contracted to provide services targeted towards youth ages 6-17 who would benefit from: personalized investment into their inherent talents and abilities; quality programming that addresses the gaps in academic enrichment; development of life skills to prepare them for the future; and increased exposure to career opportunities.

name: Community Violence Reduction Services
description: This Dataset contains the list, location and overall details about the Community Violence and Intervention Programs. These programs are contracted to provide services to communities disproportionately impacted by the Covid-19 public health emergency and the seen increase in criminal activity in Qualified Census Tracts. Organizations will provide relevant knowledge, skills and abilities in at least one (1) of the following designated areas: Conflict Resolution/Anger Management, Domestic Violence
